_comment: Example config for the Calendar Agent (TASK-29). Copy this to ~/.config/agent-manager/calendar-agent.json and replace every <PLACEHOLDER>. JSON has no comments, so explanatory keys are prefixed with _comment. They are ignored by loadConfig(). Full step-by-step setup: see docs/SETUP.md.
_comment_mcpServers: Three MCP servers the host connects. WhatsApp bridge MUST already be running and authenticated before the agent starts (see docs/SETUP.md). Gmail + Calendar share ONE Google Cloud OAuth project / one gcp-oauth.keys.json, but each server keeps its own token store and must be auth'd once each.

mcpServers._comment_whatsapp: lharries/whatsapp-mcp Python server, launched via uv. <PATH_TO_UV> = output of `which uv`. <PATH_TO_REPO> = where you cloned whatsapp-mcp. The Go bridge (whatsapp-bridge/main.go) runs SEPARATELY and is NOT started here.
mcpServers._comment_gmail: GongRzhe/Gmail-MCP-Server via npx. OAuth keys live at ~/.gmail-mcp/gcp-oauth.keys.json and the refresh token at ~/.gmail-mcp/credentials.json after running `npx @gongrzhe/server-gmail-autoauth-mcp auth`. Scope: gmail.readonly.
mcpServers._comment_calendar: @cocal/google-calendar-mcp (nspady) via npx. Reuse the SAME gcp-oauth.keys.json as Gmail. Token store: ~/.config/google-calendar-mcp/tokens.json after `npx @cocal/google-calendar-mcp auth`. Scope: calendar.

_comment_whitelist: Filters inputs BEFORE the agent reasons (AC#3). WhatsApp groups are matched by name (case-insensitive). Gmail passes if the From address contains any sender substring OR the message carries an allowed label. Empty arrays = deny-all.
